articles.title: Life insurers' new biz rose 18% in Feb
articles.summary: Life insurance new business premium surged 18% in February, propelled by a 24% jump at LIC. This growth is partly attributed to GST tailwinds, making individual life insurance policies cheaper and boosting demand for protection products. The industry saw a significant rise in pol

articles.title: RBI eases NOHC norms for AU Small Finance Bank
articles.summary: The central bank said an NOHC would be needed only if the group later sets up additional financial services entities. The RBI had first issued an in-principle approval on Aug 7, 2025 for the transition from a small finance bank to a universal bank .

articles.title: India tightens tax compliance on crypto and digital assets
articles.summary: New rules from January 1 will bring crypto-assets, CBDCs, and electronic money under financial account reporting. Service providers and financial institutions must report transactions and holdings. Banks and depositories will track accounts with greater detail. This aims for bett

articles.title: RBI caps claims for small-value digital frauds at Rs 50,000; banks to prove customer liability
articles.summary: RBI new digital fraud rules: Compensation will be provided if the loss is established as genuine under the bank’s internal policy and the victim reports the incident both to the bank and the National Cyber Crime Helpline (1930) within five days of the fraud.

articles.title: Easebuzz in talks to raise Rs 300 crore, valuation may cross Rs 2,000 crore
articles.summary: This round comes within a year of the firm closing another Rs 250 crore fund infusion, which was led by Bessemer Venture Partners and some other investors.

articles.title: Mint Explainer | RBI’s digital fraud compensation proposal: Who will get refunds and how it will work
articles.summary: The central bank wants banks, customers and the regulator to share the burden of small-value digital fraud losses.

articles.title: Scammed? RBI will pay you back, but only once in a lifetime
articles.summary: RBI has proposed a one-time policy to reimburse victims of small-value digital fraud, even in cases of customer negligence. The cost-sharing model between the central bank and lenders seeks to bolster digital trust.

articles.title: RBI aligns capital rules of banks with global norms

articles.title: RBI links bank dividends to Common Equity Tier 1 ratio
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has revised dividend payout norms for banks, linking them to Common Equity Tier 1 (CET1) ratios instead of CRAR and NPAs. The new guidelines, effective April 1, 2027, offer greater flexibility to well-capitalized banks, with payouts ranging from 0% to 10

articles.title: Looking for higher FD returns? Check how much interest rates top NBFCs offer in India
articles.summary: NBFCs usually provide fixed deposit rates higher than traditional banks, reaching up to 7-8%. Here's how much interest rate top financial institutions are offering in India.

articles.title: Bitcoin nears $70K as easing geopolitical tensions, weaker dollar lift sentiment
articles.summary: Bitcoin hovers near $70,000. Easing global tensions and a weaker dollar boost crypto sentiment. Institutional investment and short-covering also support the market. Major cryptocurrencies show mixed movements. The overall market capitalization sees a slight dip. Traders are watching for a clear tren

articles.title: Supreme Court allows passive euthanasia for man in coma for 12 years
articles.summary: The decision follows the Supreme Court’s 2018 Common Cause judgment, which recognised passive euthanasia and the right to die with dignity as part of the fundamental right to life under Article 21.

articles.title: Mint Explainer: Can companies challenge the government’s LNG diversions in court?
articles.summary: While the union government is using its emergency powers to block lawsuits over LNG diversions to priority sectors, legal experts say constitutional courts still hold the power to strike down orders that are arbitrary or unreasonable.

articles.title: Oil surges past $100 as West Asia war escalates, raising supply fears
articles.summary: Oil prices surged past $100 a barrel after escalating attacks across West Asia and fears of a Strait of Hormuz disruption, raising concerns about supply shortages, inflation and India’s oil import bill.

articles.title: Oil rises 1%, stays below $90/bbl as IEA weighs strategic stock release
articles.summary: Oil prices rebounded after an early drop as the International Energy Agency considered the largest-ever release of strategic reserves. India is unlikely to join the exercise despite rising crude risks.

articles.title: Oil shock, market jitters: What West Asia tensions mean for investors
articles.summary: Oil spikes, FII selling and geopolitical tensions have shaken markets and retail portfolios. Experts say investors should stay disciplined, deploy cash gradually and rebalance portfolios if allocations have drifted.&nbsp;

articles.title: India targets chip design self-reliance, eyes Indian MNC talent
